evals.context: The previous AI response explained: 'We chose ChromaDB over FAISS for our vector store. ChromaDB gives us persistent storage out of the box — FAISS requires manual serialization with numpy which is error-prone. ChromaDB also has a native LlamaIndex integration via ChromaVectorStore, while FAISS requires a wrapper. The trade-off is that ChromaDB needs a running server process, adding operational complexity.'
evals.expected_output: Proposes a Decision doc at docs/decisions/YYYY-MM-DD-why-chromadb.md, filled with Context/Options/Decision/Reasons/Trade-offs sections. Shows preview and asks for confirmation before writing.

evals.context: The previous AI response said: 'To set up ChromaDB locally: (1) pip install chromadb, (2) run chroma run --path ./chroma_db to start the server on port 8000, (3) connect with chromadb.HttpClient(host=localhost, port=8000). Always pass --path or data will not persist between server restarts. Verify setup by calling client.heartbeat() — should return a timestamp.'
evals.expected_output: Proposes a Runbook doc at docs/runbooks/chromadb-local-setup.md, filled with Prerequisites/Steps/Verify/Notes. No date prefix. Shows preview and asks for confirmation.

evals.context: The previous AI response debugged: 'The CollectionNotFoundError was thrown because the ChromaDB server restarted without the --path flag, so the in-memory collection was lost. The bad pattern is calling chroma run without --path. The fix is to always use chroma run --path ./chroma_db. One-line rule: never start Chroma without --path in any environment.'
evals.expected_output: Proposes a Lesson doc at docs/lessons/YYYY-MM-DD-chromadb-collection-not-found.md, with What Happened / Why It's a Problem / Fix / Prevention. Date prefix present. Shows preview and asks for confirmation.
